description_long: This house is a multi-story residential building with a steeply pitched gable roof and four prominent chimneys. The front facade features a central, rounded bay window section spanning three stories, flanked by symmetrical window arrangements and a single main entry door at ground level. The roof is covered with evenly spaced shingles, and the uppermost floor has dormer windows projecting from the roofline. The rear facade is simpler, with rows of rectangular windows and no bay projection. The side elevations are plain, with no windows or decorative elements, emphasizing the building's rectangular footprint. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from masonry or concrete, with glass windows and wooden or metal framing for doors and window sashes.
description: A tall, gabled multi-story house with a central rounded bay window, symmetrical windows, and a shingled roof with dormers and chimneys.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, three-story rounded bay window, symmetrical window rows, and detailed roof with chimneys and dormers.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain, windowless gable end and the steep pitch of the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with flat facade, symmetrical rows of rectangular windows, and dormer windows on the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain gable end and roof slope.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with central bay windows and dormers
attribute_entryway_design: Single main door with a small step, centered below the bay window, no porch or columns, simple trim.
